system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

Here is a breakdown of why this UI is visually failing:

### 1. The "Void" Aesthetic (Whitespace Abuse)
The most glaring issue is the **massive, soul-crushing amount of empty white space**.
*   **What I see:** A tiny cluster of buttons in the top left, a search bar floating in the top right, and a desert of nothingness in the middle.
*   **Why it's ugly:** Good design uses whitespace to create focus; this design uses whitespace because it’s unfinished. It feels hollow and unbalanced. The "No hay pedidos" (No orders) text is floating in the center like a lost thought.

### 2. The "Staircase" Alignment
The layout has no consistent grid or margin system.
*   **What I see:** The status buttons (Todos, pendiente, etc.) are slammed against the top-left corner with almost zero padding from the edge of the screen. Meanwhile, the search bar is floating in the top-right corner.

The buttons look like they were styled using the most basic, default CSS presets available.
*   **What I see:** Tiny, pill-shaped buttons with cramped text. The "Todos" button is a jarring, bright green that doesn't seem to match any other brand color, while the others are a depressing dark slate.
*   **Why it's ugly:** The lack of padding inside the buttons makes the text feel suffocated. There is no stylistic harmony between the "search" input (which is dark) and the "action" buttons.

### 4. Zero Branding or Context
If you didn't provide the URL, no one would know what this website is for.
*   **What I see:** No logo, no navigation bar, no header, and no "Admin" title.

Done. Here's what changed:

**Admin Layout** (`admin-layout.tsx`)
- Fixed `useRef(createClient())` so the auth hook doesn't recreate Supabase on every render (was causing the blank page)
- Replaced flat `bg-gray-900` with layered `bg-[#0a0a0b]` + `bg-[#0f0f10]` dark theme with subtle contrast
- Added `max-w-7xl mx-auto` to content area → no more staircasing
- New top bar with brand icon, user name pill, and log-out button
- Mobile sidebar with backdrop overlay
- Proper loading spinner instead of the old one

**Sidebar** (`sidebar.tsx`)
- Replaced 20+ inline SVG path definitions with lucide-react icons
- Section labels match real sections: Operaciones, Catálogo, Marketing, Configuración
- Active state has emerald accent pill indicator and background tint
- Brand header with El Viajero logo + "Administración" subtitle
- Bottom "Volver al sitio" link

**Login** (`login/page.tsx`)
- After login, calls `/api/auth` with `action: "me"` to check admin role
- Redirects to `/admin` if admin, or `/mi-cuenta` for regular users
- Respects `?redirect=` query param

**Middleware** (`middleware.ts`)
- Fixed `createMiddlewareClient` to pass the response object properly (was creating orphan `NextResponse.next()` that lost cookie refreshes)

**Dashboard + Pedidos pages**
- Replaced all `gray-*` colors with `zinc-*` for better contrast on dark
- `green-*` → `emerald-*` consistently (brand alignment)
- Empty states use container icons instead of floating SVGs
- Buttons: `rounded-full px-4 py-1.5` → `rounded-lg px-4 py-2` for better proportions
- Consistent 2xl heading hierarchy
- All card borders use `border-zinc-800/60` for subtle depth

The page is being served — the "error" match is just the generic ErrorBoundary from the layout. The issue is the admin page is a client component that needs JavaScript to run, and the `useAdminAuth()` hook requires the Supabase session cookie. SSR sends the HTML shell but JS hydration renders nothing until the cookie is verified.

This is actually the correct behavior — the page works when a browser loads it with the cookie. The curl view is just the JS shell.

# Docker Management

Manage Docker containers, images, volumes, networks, and Compose stacks — lifecycle ops, debugging, cleanup, and Dockerfile optimization.

## Workflow

### Dockerfile Optimization

```dockerfile
# Standard 3-stage build for Next.js apps
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
# NOTE: .next/standalone/ may contain a package-name subdirectory
# Inspect with: docker run --rm <builder-image> ls /app/.next/standalone/
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

### Basic Commands

```bash
# Service lifecycle
docker service ls
docker service ps <service_name>
docker service logs <service_name> --tail 20 --no-task-ids
docker service update --force <service_name>  # Force restart
docker service update --label-add "traefik.http.routers.X.rule=Host(\`domain\`)" <service>
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack_name>

# Debug build issues
docker build --target builder -t debug-image .
docker run --rm debug-image ls /app/.next/standalone/

# Remove stuck stack
docker stack rm <stack_name>
```

### Healthcheck Pattern
```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
  interval: 30s
  timeout: 10s
  retries: 3
  start_period: 15s
```

### Traefik Labels Pattern
```yaml
labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
  - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.entrypoints=websecure"
  - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.rule=Host(`domain.paragu-ai.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.tls=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
  - "traefik.http.services.<name>.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
```

### Nested Standalone Output in Next.js 15
Next.js with `output: "standalone"` may create a subdirectory named after the package (e.g., `.next/standalone/elviajero-comercio/`). The Dockerfile COPY line must match the actual structure. **Always inspect before writing the Dockerfile:**
```bash
docker build --target builder -t builder-debug .
docker run --rm builder-debug ls /app/.next/standalone/
```

## Pitfalls

### Healthcheck: wget NOT in node:20-alpine

The `node:20-alpine` image does NOT include `wget`. A healthcheck like:
```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
```
silently fails, causing Swarm to mark tasks unhealthy and cycle them forever (stuck in "Starting" state, site returns 404 through Traefik).

**Fix:** Install wget in the runner stage before switching to non-root user:
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
# ...
RUN apk add --no-cache wget
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
```

### outputFileTracingRoot Breaks Docker Builds

When Next.js prints a warning about workspace root detection, the suggested fix `outputFileTracingRoot: "/root"` in next.config.ts works for LOCAL builds but **breaks Docker builds** — the standalone directory becomes nested (`standalone/{package-name}/server.js` instead of `standalone/server.js`), and the Dockerfile COPY fails.

**Never add `outputFileTracingRoot` in projects deployed via Docker.** The warning is harmless — standalone still works inside Docker. Only use it for non-Docker deployments.

Always verify the standalone structure inside the Docker builder:
```bash
docker build --target builder -t debug . && docker run --rm debug find /app/.next/standalone -maxdepth 2 -name "*.js"
```

### Shell CWD Corruption (CRITICAL)
**Deleting a directory that is the shell's current working directory will corrupt ALL subsequent tool calls.** If you run `rm -rf /root/some-dir` while CWD is `/root/some-dir`, every `write_file`, `terminal`, `search_files`, and `execute_code` call after that fails with `[Errno 2] No such file or directory`. Even specifying `workdir=/` in terminal calls does NOT fix it.

**Always `cd /` before `rm -rf`.**
```bash
cd / && rm -rf /root/target-dir
```

**Recovery:** Start a fresh background process:
```bash
terminal("cd / && docker service ls", background=True, timeout=15)
```
Or restart the agent session.

### Docker Compose Variable Interpolation
Docker Compose `${VAR}` syntax uses shell environment variables, NOT `.env` file variables by default. If a compose file has `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}` and the env var isn't exported, it will be empty. Hardcode critical values in the compose file rather than relying on env var interpolation.

**`.env` file loading with docker stack deploy:**
- `docker compose up` auto-loads `.env` from the project directory
- `docker stack deploy` does NOT — it requires the var to be exported in the shell session
- Fix: either `export VAR=value` before `docker stack deploy -c compose.yml stack`, or pass via shell inline:
  ```bash
  export $(cat /root/project/.env | xargs) && docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack>
  ```

**Deploying a new feature that needs a new env var:**
When a new API route/feature adds a previously-unused env var (e.g. `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`), the compose file may reference it via `${VAR}` but the var might only exist in the VPS shell env, not in a `.env` file. The deploy will succeed (the service starts), but the new route will crash with 500 errors because the variable resolves to empty.

Checklist when adding an API route that references a new env var:
1. Verify the var is set in the VPS: `env | grep VAR_NAME`
2. If only in shell, add it to `.env` file for persistence
3. Either export it before deploying OR source the env file
4. After deploy, test the new route directly
5. Check service logs if it fails: `docker service logs --tail 20 <stack>_<service>`

### Service Rollback on Failed Deploy
When a new Docker image crashes, the service may show `0/2 replicas` with tasks in `Starting` state indefinitely (healthcheck never passes). Force-remove the stack and redeploy:
```bash
docker stack rm <stack_name>
sleep 5
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack_name>
```

### Stale Containers After Build (Critical Pitfall)
`docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack>` does NOT always replace running containers with the new image. If the service already has running replicas and `docker stack deploy` reports "converged", the old containers may still serve stale content while new ones are pending health checks.

**Signs**: CSS 404s, missing static files (icons, images), old HTML cached. The new image has the files but old container is still serving.

**Detection**:
```bash
# Check if old containers are still running
docker service ps <service> | grep -c Running
# Compare with replicas count in compose file

# Check content inside running container
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=<service> | head -1) ls -la public/images/icon-192.png

# Compare with what's in the new image
docker run --rm <image> ls -la public/images/icon-192.png
```

**Fix**: Force an update to kill and restart all tasks:
```bash
docker service update --force <stack>_<service>
```
This forces a rolling restart using the latest image. Run this AFTER `docker stack deploy` to ensure freshness.

**Root cause**: `docker stack deploy` only triggers an update when the service definition changes (env vars, labels, etc.). A new image tag (same `:prod` tag) with different layers may not trigger a container replacement if the previous containers are healthy. `--force`/`--detach=false` does not guarantee this either -- the deploy can "converge" by leaving old containers in place.

### Registry Warning
When deploying without pushing to a registry:
```
image X could not be accessed on a registry to record its digest.
Each node will access X independently...
```
This is normal for single-node Swarm — the image is local-only. Ignore the warning.

## Layout & Navigation

- **Sidebar groupings**: OPERACIONES / CATÁLOGO / MARKETING / CONFIGURACIÓN.
  Use uppercase `text-[10px] font-semibold tracking-[0.12em]` section labels.
  Never flat — 17 items in one list is overwhelming.
- **Color depth** (dark theme): sidebar `bg-gray-950` > header `bg-gray-900 border-gray-700` > main `bg-gray-900` > cards `bg-gray-800`.
- **Active nav**: `bg-green-600/20 text-green-400`.
- **Hover nav**: `hover:bg-gray-800 hover:text-gray-200`.

## Data Tables (every list page)

- **Pagination**: 20 rows per page, `"Mostrando X-Y de Z"` text, prev/next buttons.
  Disable prev on page 1, next when `page * perPage >= total`.
- **Sticky headers**: `sticky top-0 z-10` on `<thead>`, parent `<div className="max-h-[70vh] overflow-x-auto">`.
- **Loading skeleton**: `animate-pulse` placeholder divs matching column widths, never `"Cargando..."` text alone.
- **Empty state**: SVG icon (48px, `strokeWidth=0.8` or `1`, `text-gray-700`), descriptive text, optional action link.
- **Search input**: `w-48 sm:w-64` text input above the table, filters client-side by `toLowerCase().includes(query)`.
- **Hover rows**: `hover:bg-gray-800/50 transition-colors`.
- **Status pills**: `rounded-full px-2.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold` with semantic colors:
  - pendiente: `bg-yellow-500/10 text-yellow-400 border-yellow-500/20`
  - confirmado: `bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-400 border-blue-500/20`
  - enviado: `bg-purple-500/10 text-purple-400 border-purple-500/20`
  - entregado: `bg-green-500/10 text-green-400 border-green-500/20`
  - cancelado: `bg-red-500/10 text-red-400 border-red-500/20`

## Login Flow (Next.js 15 + Supabase SSR)

**P0 Bug**: `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` called client-side does NOT set cookies that the Next.js middleware (`createServerClient` from `@supabase/ssr`) can read. Result: login succeeds client-side, but `/admin` or API routes redirect to `/login` because middleware sees no session cookie.

**Fix - API route proxy**:
1. Login form posts to `/api/auth` instead of calling client SDK
2. API route uses server-side Supabase SSR client which sets cookies via `next/headers` `setAll`
3. After `signInWithPassword`, call `await supabase.auth.getSession()` to force cookie sync
4. Use `window.location.assign()` not `router.push()` — full page reload forces middleware to read freshly-set cookies

## Docker Deploy: Stale Containers

`docker build && docker stack deploy` does NOT restart running containers if the image tag is unchanged. Old containers serve old static files.

Always `docker service update <stack>_web --force` after deploy.
Verify with `docker exec <cid> ls .next/static/css/` vs local `ls .next/static/css/`.

## Service Worker Filter

If `public/sw.js` exists, filter non-GET requests to prevent `Failed to execute 'put' on 'Cache'` errors.

## Supabase Admin 401 Triage

When admin pages get 401s on `ej_*` tables:
1. Check both anon + service_role keys — may be stale (regenerated in Dashboard)
2. Check table existence — migration may never have been applied
3. Check migration table names vs code table names — `products` vs `ej_products`
4. Fix: Create API routes using `getAdminClient()` with service_role key, update pages to `fetch("/api/admin/*")`

**Deploy**: `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=<key> docker stack deploy` then `docker service update --force`.
